description: Integrate all new modules into the system entry point, update README.md with all new features, and ensure final build compiles cleanly.
steps: Update system/mk_entry.cpp to #include the new NGF engine (ai_core/ngf/ngf_engine.cpp), coding engine (ai_core/coding_engine/code_writer.cpp), conversation modules (ai_core/conversation/personality_engine.cpp, memory_manager.cpp, context_tracker.cpp), web scraper (network/web_scraper.cpp, network/realtime_search.cpp), and task engine (ai_core/task_engine/reminder_system.cpp, task_planner.cpp). Add them to the MKSystem struct and wire them into the REPL command handlers. Add new commands: /code, /remind, /plan, /project.
Update the Makefile if needed to ensure the new directories are found by the source file discovery

Update README.md at /projects/sandbox/MK_os-2/README.md with all new features: NGF engine description, LLM voice module, full coding engine, conversation personality, memory manager, web scraper, real-time search, reminder system, task planner, project indexer, and the massive 6GB+ knowledge base. Update the architecture diagram, file structure table, stats section (new file count, line count), and What MK OS Can Do section.
Run make all to verify everything compiles together cleanly
acceptance_criteria: New commands (/code, /remind, /plan, /project) are available in the REPL
make all produces mk_os binary without errors
The architecture diagram in README is updated
verification: Run 'make all' from /projects/sandbox/MK_os-2/MK_OS/ and confirm it produces the mk_os binary
Run 'grep -c FEAT /projects/sandbox/MK_os-2/README.md' or similar to confirm README has new content
